title: centaurea alba subsp. brunnea
intro: Centaurea alba subsp. brunnea, also known as the yellow knapweed, is a perennial plant species in the family Asteraceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The health benefits of Centaurea alba subsp. brunnea include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote the healing of wounds. The plant's aerial parts are often used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
